verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Rey Cortiço Early morning — 08:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço looks like the best bodyboard option for early morning — 08:00.  This should feel like a fun progression session rather than a heavy one.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: Rey Cortiço looks like a rare window today, especially around the early morning (around 08:00), with decent, glassy wind and waves that should be just enough to punch. I’m planning to check the Surfline and MEO Beachcam before leaving, since the sandbanks can shift fast and this spot is wind-exposed. The mid tide is likely to be the most interesting, so I’ll go with that timing if the cams look as good as expected.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 71 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 74 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 57/100, and glassy conditions.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 60 kJ, 8 s period, lully 29/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 90 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and glassy conditions.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Expect: it is less reliable than the main bay spots, so weak swell or short period can make it feel soft.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
